speaker: RICHARD
text: Brother, though I be youngest, give me leave.

speaker: EDWARD
text: No, I can better play the orator.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: But I have reasons strong and forcible.

speaker: YORK
text: Why, how now, sons and brother, at a strife?

speaker: YORK
text: What is your quarrel? How began it first?

speaker: EDWARD
text: No quarrel, but a slight contention.

speaker: YORK
text: About what?

speaker: RICHARD
text: About that which concerns your Grace and us:

speaker: RICHARD
text: The crown of England, father, which is yours.

speaker: YORK
text: Mine, boy? Not till King Henry be dead.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your right depends not on his life or death.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Now you are heir; therefore enjoy it now.

speaker: EDWARD
text: By giving the house of Lancaster leave to breathe,

speaker: EDWARD
text: It will outrun you, father, in the end.

speaker: YORK
text: I took an oath that he should quietly reign.

speaker: EDWARD
text: But for a kingdom any oath may be broken.

speaker: EDWARD
text: I would break a thousand oaths to reign one year.

speaker: RICHARD
text: No, God forbid your Grace should be forsworn.

speaker: YORK
text: I shall be, if I claim by open war.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll prove the contrary, if you’ll hear me speak.

speaker: YORK
text: Thou canst not, son; it is impossible.

speaker: RICHARD
text: An oath is of no moment, being not took

speaker: RICHARD
text: Before a true and lawful magistrate

speaker: RICHARD
text: That hath authority over him that swears.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Henry had none, but did usurp the place.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then, seeing ’twas he that made you to depose,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your oath, my lord, is vain and frivolous.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Therefore, to arms! And, father, do but think

speaker: RICHARD
text: How sweet a thing it is to wear a crown,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Within whose circuit is Elysium

speaker: RICHARD
text: And all that poets feign of bliss and joy.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why do we linger thus? I cannot rest

speaker: RICHARD
text: Until the white rose that I wear be dyed

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even in the lukewarm blood of Henry’s heart.

speaker: YORK
text: Richard, enough. I will be king or die.—

speaker: YORK
text: Brother, thou shalt to London presently,

speaker: YORK
text: And whet on Warwick to this enterprise.—

speaker: YORK
text: Thou, Richard, shalt to the Duke of Norfolk

speaker: YORK
text: And tell him privily of our intent.—

speaker: YORK
text: You, Edward, shall unto my Lord Cobham,

speaker: YORK
text: With whom the Kentishmen will willingly rise;

speaker: YORK
text: In them I trust, for they are soldiers

speaker: YORK
text: Witty, courteous, liberal, full of spirit.

speaker: YORK
text: While you are thus employed, what resteth more

speaker: YORK
text: But that I seek occasion how to rise,

speaker: YORK
text: And yet the King not privy to my drift,

speaker: YORK
text: Nor any of the house of Lancaster.

speaker: YORK
text: But stay, what news? Why com’st thou in such post?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Queen with all the northern earls and lords

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Intend here to besiege you in your castle.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: She is hard by with twenty thousand men.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And therefore fortify your hold, my lord.

speaker: YORK
text: Ay, with my sword. What, think’st thou that we fear them?—

speaker: YORK
text: Edward and Richard, you shall stay with me;

speaker: YORK
text: My brother Montague shall post to London.

speaker: YORK
text: Let noble Warwick, Cobham, and the rest,

speaker: YORK
text: Whom we have left Protectors of the King,

speaker: YORK
text: With powerful policy strengthen themselves

speaker: YORK
text: And trust not simple Henry nor his oaths.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: Brother, I go. I’ll win them, fear it not.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: And thus most humbly I do take my leave.

speaker: YORK
text: Sir John and Sir Hugh Mortimer, mine uncles,

speaker: YORK
text: You are come to Sandal in a happy hour.

speaker: YORK
text: The army of the Queen mean to besiege us.

speaker:
SIR
               
              JOHN
text: She shall not need; we’ll meet her in the field.

speaker: YORK
text: What, with five thousand men?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, with five hundred, father, for a need.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A woman’s general; what should we fear?

speaker: EDWARD
text: I hear their drums. Let’s set our men in order,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And issue forth and bid them battle straight.

speaker: YORK
text: Five men to twenty: though the odds be great,

speaker: YORK
text: I doubt not, uncle, of our victory.

speaker: YORK
text: Many a battle have I won in France

speaker: YORK
text: Whenas the enemy hath been ten to one.

speaker: YORK
text: Why should I not now have the like success?
